chunks.page_summary: This page details the integration of Rheonics SRV viscometer and SRD density-viscosity meters with Ignition SCADA, emphasizing a streamlined approach for real-time process monitoring and data visualization without the need for a PLC.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

Rheonics sensor integration with Ignition SCADA

Modified on: Fri, 4 Oct, 2024 at 6:23 PM

1. What products are involved?
Rheonics SRV viscometer (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-viscometer-srv/) and SRD density-viscosity meter (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-density-meter-srd/).
(https://rheonics.com/products/inline-viscometer-srv/)

2. What is the purpose of this article?
This article outlines the steps for integrating Rheonics Smart Module Electronics (SME) with Ignition SCADA by Inductive Automation®. Rheonics SME provides advanced capabilities for process monitoring and control, and when paired with Ignition, it offers a modern, flexible solution for SCADA systems. Ignition’s open and scalable platform allows for seamless process control, data tracking, and real-time analysis, addressing many of the limitations found in traditional SCADA systems.

With Rheonics sensors, you can retrieve and visualize sensor data within minutes—no PLC needed. Fully compatible with the Ignition Perspective platform, Rheonics offers a streamlined way to bring real-time sensor data into Ignition, providing instant insights without complex programming or scripting.

Figure 1. Connection Diagram with Ignition.
key_facts: The Rheonics SRV viscometer and SRD density-viscosity meter are key products involved in this integration.
The article describes the integration of Rheonics Smart Module Electronics (SME) with Ignition SCADA by Inductive Automation®.
Rheonics SME offers advanced capabilities for process monitoring and control.
Ignition, in conjunction with Rheonics SME, provides a modern, flexible, open, and scalable solution for SCADA systems.
Ignition's platform enables seamless process control, data tracking, and real-time analysis.
The integration addresses limitations commonly found in traditional SCADA systems.
Rheonics sensors allow for retrieving and visualizing sensor data within minutes.
The integration eliminates the need for a PLC.
Rheonics sensor solutions are fully compatible with the Ignition Perspective platform.
The system provides instant insights from real-time sensor data without complex programming or scripting.

Mapped data for Ignition can be exported from a specified Rheonics support URL.

The configuration interface allows for importing, exporting, and saving Modbus settings for sensor variables.
Sensor data can be accessed and verified through an OPC Quick Client application.

The Modbus configuration supports various data types, including Float, Int16, and Double registers.

4. How do you use the Rheonics templates available in the ignition exchange?

The ready-to-use Rheonics templates allow users to quickly deploy dashboards for Rheonics sensors SRV and SRD, enabling real-time data visualization. Available on the Ignition Exchange, the templates include UDT tag definitions and Perspective views for multiple Rheonics sensors. Simply create a UDT instance for each sensor, and you're ready to display and analyze the data directly in Ignition.
key_facts: The Rheonics Control Panel software is version 5.4.0.2.
The system is currently running with status code E00.
Data logging is currently stopped.
Rheonics provides ready-to-use templates for Ignition to facilitate quick deployment of dashboards for SRV and SRD sensors.
These templates enable real-time data visualization and are accessible on the Ignition Exchange.
The templates include User Defined Type (UDT) tag definitions and Perspective views tailored for multiple Rheonics sensors.
Users can display and analyze sensor data directly in Ignition by creating a UDT instance for each sensor.

key_facts: Rheonics provides instrument interface templates for Viscosity meter SRV and Density meter SRD on the Ignition Exchange.
The available templates are version 1.0.0, updated on October 1, 2024, and have a size of 37.69 KB.
The templates require Ignition Platform version 8.1.43.
The process involves selecting the Rheonics Instrument interface, downloading the templates, and then importing them into the Ignition designer tool.
The templates allow for monitoring parameters like viscosity, density, kinematic viscosity, and temperature from SRV and SRD sensors.
The Rheonics_SRV template, when opened in the designer, shows viscosity and temperature readings.
diagram_labels: Viscosity meter SRV and Density meter SRD

chunks.page_summary: This page provides step-by-step instructions for importing Rheonics SRV and SRD templates into Ignition Designer, explaining their utility for real-time monitoring of fluid viscosity, density, and temperature to ensure consistent quality in industrial processes.
chunks.extracted_text:
5. Select the Zip file that you downloaded from the ignition exchange page.
(Figure 17. Selecting UDT templates zip. displays a screenshot of Ignition Designer with an 'Open' dialog showing 'Rheonics_2024-10-01_1413.zip' selected, alongside the Viscometer SRV template displaying a viscosity of '0.0516 cP'.)
6. Add the templates by clicking on the top of the import button.
(Figure 18 Adding devices. displays a screenshot of Ignition Designer with an 'Import' dialog, showing 'Vision Templates', 'Rheonics_SRD', and 'Rheonics_SRV' selected for import.)

5. SRV and SRD templates
The SRV and SRD templates include the necessary parameter outputs for the correct monitoring of fluids. These templates ensure that all critical data—such as viscosity, density, and temperature—is captured and displayed accurately, enabling operators to track fluid behavior in real-time. This is crucial for maintaining consistent quality in processes like mixing, pumping, and manufacturing, where precise control of fluid properties is essential.
key_facts: Rheonics SRV and SRD templates provide parameter outputs essential for monitoring fluids.
The templates capture and display critical data including viscosity, density, and temperature accurately.
Operators can track fluid behavior in real-time using these templates.
Real-time fluid tracking is crucial for maintaining consistent quality in industrial processes.
Precise control of fluid properties is essential in mixing, pumping, and manufacturing processes.
diagram_labels: Check the resources from this project to import, and press Import

chunks.page_summary: This page illustrates the output parameters and example readings for Rheonics SRV viscometer and SRD density meter sensors.
chunks.extracted_text:
By providing structured and reliable data, the templates help ensure that fluids are monitored effectively, allowing operators to identify any variations in process conditions.

Sensor Type: SRV
Parameter output: Viscosity, Temperature
Example: A Rheonics Viscometer SRV sensor (labeled SRV 1) displays a Viscosity of 0.0532 cP and a Temperature of 21.83 °C.

Sensor Type: SRD
Parameter output: Density, Viscosity, Kinematic Viscosity, Temperature
Example: A Rheonics Density Meter SRD sensor (labeled SRD 1) displays a Density of 0.8800 g/cc, Viscosity of 0.0883 cP, Kinematic Viscosity of 0.0947 cSt, and a Temperature of 22.25 °C.
key_facts: Rheonics sensors provide structured and reliable data.
The data helps ensure effective fluid monitoring.
Operators can use the data to identify variations in process conditions.
The SRV sensor outputs Viscosity and Temperature.
The SRD sensor outputs Density, Viscosity, Kinematic Viscosity, and Temperature.
